title: Replace raw tyre-age heuristics with online latent tyre-state and cliff features
summary: A recent Formula 1 state-space paper models lap time using latent tire pace and treats pit stops as structural state resets.
rationale: A recent Formula 1 state-space paper models lap time using latent tire pace and treats pit stops as structural state resets. That directly matches this problem: the useful signal is not just `TyreLife`, but whether degradation has entered a latent ‘cliff’ regime for the current stint.
implementation_hint: Fit a lightweight causal filter per driver-race-stint using only past/current laps to estimate latent degradation level, degradation slope, and residual pace loss after accounting for race progress. Add features such as posterior slope, posterior variance, residual-vs-expected lap delta, filtered cliff flag, and time-since-reset. Feed those features into the existing tree models instead of building a full standalone Bayesian predictor.
expected_effect: Can outperform simple tyre/stint arithmetic if the public data’s degradation column is noisy or incomplete, especially in long dry stints.
risk: Leakage is easy if future laps are used during smoothing; use strictly forward filtering, not full-sequence smoothing.
